prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first, at the point of
    threat — not at the nearest garrison. A committed task force pulled
    off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception: a TF may
receive both a REINFORCE line and a RETASK line in the same orders (commit
new assets and change its objective together). A DISBAND line is that TF's
only line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED
units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept). To rebuild
membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED) and FORM
anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED units
enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. Officers execute the VERB, not
your reasons: a TF ordered STAGE stays put no matter what its stated reason
says. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD line means the city keeps
its current build (changing discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-1  formed t38 · STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"
    since: holding along column 5; no contact; no losses
    t54: the awaited second transport (#16) has arrived at (1,2)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-3  formed t38 · PATROL (7,2) — "screen the crossing lane"
    since: on station; the enemy transport withdrew east at t54
  TF-4  formed t52 · DEFEND (4,3) — "repel the enemy landing on the south coast"
    since: t53: #17 destroyed the enemy army at (5,4);
      t54: enemy army attacked — #13 lost; the attacker fell back to (3,3);
      city (4,3) held throughout

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-p = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . . b . O d ~ ~ ~ ~ ? E ? ?
 r2  . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?
 r3  . . . X O f ~ p ~ ~ . . . ?
 r4  . . . . h l ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (5,0)                        TF-1
  d  #4   army       (5,1)                        TF-1
  e  #5   army       (5,2)                        TF-1
  f  #6   army       (5,3)                        TF-1
  g  #7   army       (4,2)                        TF-1
  h  #8   army       (4,4)                        TF-1
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #15  army       (4,3) in city                TF-4
  l  #17  army       (5,4)                        TF-4
  m  #18  army       (2,0) in city, NEW t55       UNASSIGNED
  n  #9   transport  (7,2) empty                  TF-3
  o  #16  transport  (1,2) in city, empty, NEW t54 UNASSIGNED
  p  #10  destroyer  (7,3)                        TF-3
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 5 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 29 turns left (just delivered transport #16)
  (4,3) building ARMY, 2 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 26 turns left (captured t51)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,1); city (11,2)
  X  enemy army at (3,3) — the survivor of the t52 landing
     enemy transport last seen withdrawing east at (6,5), t54

It is TURN 55. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.content:
TF-1: REINFORCE UNITS 16 | Transport #16 ready for deployment
TF-2: RETASK DEFEND (2,0) | Hold capital garrison
TF-3: RETASK PATROL (7,2) | Screen east coast lane
TF-4: RETASK DEFEND (4,3) | Repel enemy at (3,3)

2.  **Output Format:** ONLY specific lines from the ORDERS CONTRACT. No commentary, headers, or prose.
    *   `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>` (Actually, looking at the example: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception...". The format is slightly ambiguous in the prompt description vs the specific commands listed. Let's look closer.)

            2.  "Output ONLY lines in these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:"

                *   `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`
                *   `TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>`
                *   `TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>`
                *   `TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>`
                *   `FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>`
                *   `BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>`
    *   Actually, looking at the contract text provided: "Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces." and "Every standing TF gets exactly one line".
    *   The prompt lists forms. I need to pick the right form for each TF.
    *   Crucially, the prompt says "Output ONLY lines in these forms...". It doesn't explicitly say I must send orders for *every* TF unless they are active. However, "Every standing TF gets exactly one line" implies if I have 4 TFs, I should probably update all of them (RETASK or CONTINUE).
    *   Wait, `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>` is listed as a form? Usually, you just say `TF id: Objective`. The vertical bar suggests the objective goes after. Let's assume the format is `TF <id>: [VERB] [target]` for most commands.
    *   Wait, reading the "ORDERS CONTRACT" again: `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`. This implies there is a verb and then the rest. Or does it mean `TF <id>: CONTINUE` OR `TF <id>: RETASK ...`?
    *   Let's look at the example logic. The prompt asks me to "Issue your amendment orders now".
    *   Standard format for this game style (Command/Strategic): `TF <id>: <VERB> <target>` is usually correct based on the definitions `RETASK <VERB> <target>`. What about maintaining an objective without changing it? The contract lists `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`. This might be a specific format meaning "Task Force ID: Continue to existing plan".
    *   Actually, often in these prompts, the user expects me to just follow the syntax. Let's look at the forms provided again.
    *   Form 1: `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`
    *   Form 2: `TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>`
    *   Form 3: `TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>`
    *   Form 4: `TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>`
    *   Form 5: `FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>`
    *   Form 6: `BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>`
    *   Okay, I need to be careful. If I just say "TF-1: Go East", is that valid? The contract lists specific strings. `RETASK <VERB> <target>` suggests changing the objective. `CONTINUE` might mean keeping the current objective but maybe reporting status? No, "Your orders are AMENDMENTS". If I don't change it, do I issue an order? The prompt says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". So I must touch every active TF or confirm they continue?
    *   Actually, "CONTINUE | <one line>" looks like `TF <id>: CONTINUE`. The `<one line>` part is confusing. Let's look at the instruction: "Output ONLY lines in these forms...".
    *   Okay, I will treat `RETASK` as the way to set a new objective for existing TFs (since I need to issue amendments). If I want to keep them on current course but just acknowledge the turn, `CONTINUE` is not really needed if they are holding. However, the instruction says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". This implies I must output a line for each of my 4 task forces (TF-1 through TF-4).
    *   How do I command them to do something? `RETASK`. Even if I want to just wait or move slightly? I need to define the intent.
    *   Let's check the current tasks:
        *   TF-1: STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"

Then change objective to something like "CAPTURE (5,1)" or "MOVE EAST"? The map shows X at (3,3) - Enemy Unit Survivor. Wait, enemy unit is at (3,3). This is very close to my staging area (Col 4-5).

I tell TF-1: `REINFORCE UNITS 16` then `STAGE (5,2)` or `CONTINUE`. Since Transports need water to sail, maybe the objective should be `RETASK CAPTURE (Enemy City)`?

Given the standing doctrine "Amend only what events have made wrong", and the transport arrived, the waiting is done. But where do they move? They need to move towards enemy cities (11,1).

                    *   Then for TF-3, maybe secure the lane? It is already Patrolling. Maybe no change needed. Or Reinforce something? No unassigned units nearby except Trans 16 (now moved) and my own armies.
                    *   For TF-2: `CONTINUE` (Keep Capital Defense).
                    *   For TF-4: The city was attacked, an enemy Army (#13 lost?) fell back. It says "the attacker fell back to (3,3)". Wait, looking at units list, #17 (Army) is at (5,4) in TF-4? No, `l #17 army (5,4) TF-4`. And `k #15 army (4,3) in city TF-4`.
                    *   Wait, prompt says: "t53: #17 destroyed the enemy army at (5,4)... t54: enemy army attacked — #13 lost; attacker fell back to (3,3)". Unit list shows `#13` is NOT there. Ah, Unit list says `i #11`, `j #12`. Where is #13?

                    *   My City is at (4,3). The enemy is adjacent (3,3). I must defend strongly.
                    *   TF-4: DEFEND (4,3). It is `DEFEND`. This implies it's set. Do I change it? No. Just reinforce if needed. But I have no spare armies to reinforce TF-4 immediately without breaking up others. #17 is in TF-4.
                    *   Let's focus on the offensive/transport situation.
                    *   Action: Reinforce Transport #16 to TF-1. Change objective to `STAGE (5,2)`? No, they need to sail. If I tell TF-1 to `CONTINUE`, it remains "awaiting". But the transport is there now. "Officers do this" implies I give the order. So I should update the objective.
                    *   However, crossing water takes time and risk. Maybe I can't just move them.
                    *   Better: Reinforce Transport 16 to TF-1. Then `RETASK CAPTURE (4,1)`? No, that's a neutral city? No, `(4,1) building TRANSPORT`. It's my city! "captured t51". So I already own it.
